The PR adds a `routeReply` fallback for compaction `notifyUser` notices when `onBlockReply` is unavailable and adds auto-reply regression tests.

PR surface: Source +51, Tests +144. Total +195 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main returns from `sendCompactionNotice` whenever `notifyUser` is true but `opts.onBlockReply` is absent; I did not run a live channel reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should require the queued/proactive code repair plus real behavior proof; automation should not proceed while the contributor proof gate is mock-only.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): route compaction notices without block replies
target.body:
## Summary
- Route `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser` notices through the originating channel when an active block-reply callback is unavailable.
- Preserve the existing `onBlockReply` path for live streaming turns.
- Keep notice delivery non-fatal if there is no routable origin or the fallback send fails.

## Linked context
Closes #88933

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: compaction notices with `notifyUser: true` were silently dropped when a compaction event ran without `opts.onBlockReply`.
- Real environment tested: Linux contributor checkout; focused OpenClaw auto-reply tests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- Evidence after fix: targeted regression passes and asserts the no-`onBlockReply` path calls `routeReply` with the compaction notice, origin channel, destination, `replyKind: "block"`, and `mirror: false`.
- Observed result after fix: `agent-runner-execution.test.ts` passed 153 tests.
- What was not tested: a live WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord compaction delivery.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this contributor environment cannot safely trigger a real user-channel compaction notice without using private local channel/account state.
- Before evidence: current source returned early from `sendCompactionNotice` whenever `params.opts?.onBlockReply` was absent.

## Tests and validation
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
- `git diff --check -- src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`

## Risk checklist
Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `Yes`
Highest-risk area: fallback delivery sends an additional notice through the origin route when block streaming is unavailable. The mitigation is to require a routable origin, avoid transcript mirroring for the notice, keep failures non-fatal, and preserve the old callback path when present.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review. Waiting on CI and any maintainer-requested live channel proof.

target.title: fix: complete preserveKeys implementation for session maintenance
target.body:
## Problem

PR #81303 was closed but the preserveKeys implementation was incomplete. The ClawSweeper review identified that while the preserveKeys field was added to types and Zod schema, it was not being used by the actual maintenance callers.

## Root Cause

The preserveKeys configuration was defined but not wired through to the session maintenance functions:
- Load-time maintenance (store-load.ts) did not pass preserveKeys to prune/cap functions
- Cleanup service (cleanup-service.ts) did not pass preserveKeys to prune/cap functions
- This meant configured preserveKeys were ignored during session maintenance

## Solution

This PR completes the preserveKeys implementation by:

1. **Fixed load-time maintenance** (src/config/sessions/store-load.ts):
   - Build preserveKeys set from maintenance configuration
   - Pass preserveKeys to both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount

2. **Fixed cleanup service** (src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts):
   - Build preserveKeys set from both active key and maintenance configuration
   - Pass preserveKeys to both pruneStaleEntries and capEntryCount

3. **Added comprehensive test suite** (src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts):
   - Tests for pruneStaleEntries with preserveKeys
   - Tests for capEntryCount with preserveKeys
   - Tests for combined prune and cap operations
   - Edge cases: empty sets, multiple keys, etc.

## Changes

- **src/config/sessions/store-load.ts**: Wire preserveKeys into load-time maintenance
- **src/config/sessions/cleanup-service.ts**: Wire preserveKeys into cleanup service
- **src/config/sessions/store.preserve-keys.test.ts**: Add comprehensive test coverage

## Testing

✅ All existing tests pass
✅ New preserveKeys test suite passes (178 lines of tests)
✅ Manual testing with configuration

## Impact

- **User Experience**: Users can now configure preserveKeys and they will actually work
- **Backward Compatible**: preserveKeys is optional, existing configs work unchanged
- **All Maintenance Paths**: preserveKeys now honored in writer, load-time, and cleanup maintenance

Fixes #80853

---

This PR replaces #81303 which was closed. This version is based on the latest upstream main branch and includes all necessary maintenance caller integration.
